title: [InstCombine][foldPHIArgGEPIntoPHI] Early return for const vector index for gep inst.
summary:
The developers generalized a GEP folding optimization by relaxing a type check from `ConstantInt` to `Constant`. The original pattern only allowed integer constants for GEP indices, but the test case revealed that vector-of-constant GEPs (e.g., `<16 x i32> zeroinitializer`) were being missed. By broadening the condition to accept any `Constant` (including vector constants and splats), the transformation now handles vectorized GEPs uniformly, enabling more aggressive folding of PHI-of-GEP patterns.

Key insight: The optimization's core requirement is constant equivalence across PHI inputs, not specifically integer constants. This change preserves safety (struct indices remain constant) while extending coverage to vectorized code. When generalizing similar patterns, identify the minimal necessary constraint—here, constant-ness—rather than over-specifying the operand type.
